delayed_cancelled.scope: US facilities with an original online date (Start of operations) in 2026-2028

cited_events.quote: Google withdrew its rezoning petition for the $1bn, 468-acre Franklin Township site after community pushback, pre-empting a council vote it was set to lose.
St. Joseph County Council denied the rezoning 7-2 for a ~$12-13bn, ~1,000-acre campus near the AWS New Carlisle site after ~10 hours of opposition.
QTS dropped its $2bn, ~800-acre Porter County scheme: 'the conditions proposed would be economically challenging.'
'Agincourt has agreed to withdraw from the project and to release its option on the land' (Mayor Jon Costas).
Surge withdrew its ~775-acre Hancock County rezoning after opposition; it is now eyeing Henry County instead.
The ~2,100-acre, gigawatt-scale 'world's largest' campus 'is officially dead' after the last developer dropped its appeal and the zoning was ruled invalid.
Microsoft withdrew its Caledonia rezoning request; its separate $3.3bn Mount Pleasant campus continues (a later phase paused for redesign).
Tract pulled its ~400-acre plan (on former Dale Earnhardt farmland) from the agenda after opposition, including from the Earnhardt family.
Oracle and OpenAI ended plans for a ~600MW expansion of the Abilene flagship; the core ~1.2GW campus continues (Oracle disputes 'cancellation' framing).
Crusoe withdrew from the 1.8GW (scalable to 10GW) project; Tallgrass Energy is seeking a replacement tenant.
Data-center capacity expansion cancelled; investment cut ~87% ($104M -> $13M) citing 'unforeseen circumstances and technical challenges'.
'Our base case moved first power off 2027 entirely and out to 2029' - gas-pipeline permitting, a state right-of-way denial and FERC review.
'We have paused early construction work for this second phase while we evaluate scope and recent changes in technology.' Phase 1 continues.
Microsoft paused ~$1bn across three Licking County sites; two of the three reverted to farmland use.
Amazon suspended its Becker plan amid a fight over Minnesota's data-center tax exemption and a backup-generator permit ruling.

cited_macro.headline: ~25 US data-center projects cancelled in 2025 (vs 6 in 2024); Indiana ~8, ~5 GW
cited_macro.detail: Cancellations roughly quadrupled year-on-year on power/water limits and local opposition; Indiana tied Virginia for the most (~8 each), with a reported ~5 GW cancelled in Indiana alone.

cited_macro.headline: Microsoft: ~200 MW of US leases cancelled, up to ~2 GW deferred (US+Europe)
cited_macro.detail: TD Cowen: ~200MW of US colocation leases cancelled (Feb 2025) with at least two private operators, rising to up to 2GW deferred/cancelled plus >1GW of expired LOIs. Microsoft says it stays 'well positioned to meet increasing demand'.

cited_macro.headline: AWS: paused a portion of colocation leasing
cited_macro.detail: Wells Fargo: AWS paused some colocation leasing discussions ('particularly international ones'); signed leases were not cancelled. AWS calls it 'routine capacity management'.

cited_macro.headline: $130B / 75+ US buildouts blocked or delayed in Q1 2026
cited_macro.detail: Data Center Watch: more than 75 projects worth ~$130bn blocked or delayed in the first four months of 2026 amid bipartisan local opposition over power and water costs.

cited_macro.headline: Caveat: 'more than half of 2026 US DCs cancelled/delayed' is disputed
cited_macro.detail: SemiAnalysis argues the 'half of 2026 capacity is cancelled' framing is a denominator error - its own year-end 2026 NA self-build forecast moved only ~1% (colo <5%). Much of the slip is delay (e.g. an electrical-gear shortage), not demand collapse.
